name: Playwright MCP

auth.notes: MCP server is exposed on loopback (127.0.0.1:8931) with --allowed-hosts restricted to localhost:8931,127.0.0.1:8931. No auth on the MCP endpoint itself; authentication to navigated sites is handled by the browser context (cookies, storage state).

tools.name: browser_click
tools.description: Click an element on the current page (with optional modifier keys / mouse button).

tools.name: browser_close
tools.description: Close the active page / tab.

tools.name: browser_console_messages
tools.description: Return console log entries captured for the current page.

tools.name: browser_drag
tools.description: Drag-and-drop between two elements.

tools.name: browser_drop
tools.description: Drop files or MIME data onto a target element.

tools.name: browser_evaluate
tools.description: Execute a JavaScript expression in the page context. Effectively in-page code execution.

tools.name: browser_file_upload
tools.description: Upload files into a page file input.

tools.name: browser_fill_form
tools.description: Fill multiple form fields in a single call.

tools.name: browser_handle_dialog
tools.description: Accept or dismiss browser dialogs (alert / confirm / prompt).

tools.name: browser_hover
tools.description: Hover the pointer over an element.

tools.name: browser_navigate
tools.description: Navigate the current tab to a URL.

tools.name: browser_navigate_back
tools.description: Navigate the current tab one step back in history.

tools.name: browser_network_request
tools.description: Return the full details of an individual captured network request.

tools.name: browser_network_requests
tools.description: List captured network requests for the current page (with filters).

tools.name: browser_press_key
tools.description: Press a keyboard key.

tools.name: browser_resize
tools.description: Resize the browser viewport.

tools.name: browser_run_code_unsafe
tools.description: Execute arbitrary Playwright code against the running browser. Vendor explicitly labels this 'unsafe'; effectively RCE-equivalent within the container's browser context.

tools.name: browser_select_option
tools.description: Select one or more options in a <select> element.

tools.name: browser_snapshot
tools.description: Return an accessibility-tree snapshot of the current page (preferred over screenshots for LLM consumption).

tools.name: browser_tabs
tools.description: Manage browser tabs (list / create / close / select).

tools.name: browser_take_screenshot
tools.description: Capture a PNG/JPEG screenshot of the current page.

tools.name: browser_type
tools.description: Type text into an editable element.

tools.name: browser_wait_for
tools.description: Wait for text to appear / disappear or for a fixed duration.

retention: None vendor-side: the tool is an OCI image we run locally. Browser state is ephemeral per-session because --isolated is set; no persistent profile, cookies, or storage state is written to disk. Container-side artifacts (screenshots, traces) live in /tmp/playwright-output and are removed with the container.
compensating_controls: Run with --isolated so no persistent browser profile, cookies, or storage state is retained across sessions.
Restrict navigable origins via PLAYWRIGHT_MCP_ALLOWED_ORIGINS, derived from playwright.allowedDomains in proxy-config.yml.
Bind the HTTP MCP server to loopback only (127.0.0.1:8931) and gate it with --allowed-hosts localhost:8931,127.0.0.1:8931.
In-sandbox mode forces all browser egress through the sandbox HTTP proxy (host.docker.internal:3128) with the proxy CA cert injected into Chromium's NSS DB, giving an inspectable choke point.

Pin the upstream image by digest (mcr.microsoft.com/playwright/mcp:v0.0.75@sha256:d238ec7bc98cc4e22df0696d6031dad5b8a4b46781f4f0abaa3bfadeedb43b9a) so an upstream tag-overwrite cannot silently alter the surface; the digest is the multi-arch OCI index, so amd64 and arm64 are both bound.
Deny browser_evaluate and browser_run_code_unsafe via managed settings.json so the model cannot drive in-page or arbitrary Playwright code execution without an explicit approval-gated un-deny.
Container runs --headless --no-sandbox in an ephemeral container; no persistent volumes are mounted in sandbox mode.

research_gaps.reason: Recorded as true because `docker buildx imagetools inspect mcr.microsoft.com/playwright/mcp:v0.0.75` returns OCI image-index manifests carrying Docker BuildKit attestation manifests (vnd.docker.reference.type: attestation-manifest) for both linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 — i.e. SLSA provenance and SBOM are attached at build time. We did NOT verify a Sigstore / cosign signature against a Microsoft identity; sigstore therefore stays false.

Microsoft GitHub org membership 2FA enforcement is not externally verifiable; treated as n/a-vendor-hosted because the artefact is published through Microsoft's enterprise registry rather than an individual account.

The image accepts opt-in capability flags (--caps=vision|pdf|devtools|network|storage|testing) that expose additional tools (e.g. browser_pdf_save, browser_*_storage_*, browser_route, browser_mouse_*_xy, browser_start_tracing). The current compose command does not pass --caps, so only the default 'core' set is exposed; these additional tools are out of scope for this snapshot and would surface as security-relevant deltas if the deployment opts in.

Self-hosted container; there is no vendor-side audit log. Recorded as false. FMFX must rely on container stdout, sandbox proxy access logs, and Claude Code's own tool-use transcripts for after-the-fact investigation.

mcp_specific.name_collision_notes: Tool names are uniformly browser_*-prefixed. No collision risk identified against currently-adopted MCPs (datadog-mcp's tools are search_*/get_*/aggregate_*-prefixed). If a future MCP exposes browser_* tools (e.g. a competing browser-automation server), the model could be confused into routing calls to the wrong server; revisit at that point.

credentials.storage_location: in-memory only (no persistent profile because --isolated is set)
credentials.revocation_mechanism: Stop / remove the container (docker compose down). There is no shared API key or OAuth grant to rotate.

local_execution.filesystem_access_notes: The container process spawns headless Chromium and writes per-session outputs to /tmp/playwright-output (sandbox mode) or the playwright-data named volume (local mode). No host filesystem is bind-mounted. The model can drive in-page JavaScript via browser_evaluate and arbitrary Playwright code via browser_run_code_unsafe; both are denied at the managed settings.json layer in the approved configuration.

indirect_injection_vectors: browser_snapshot — accessibility tree text from arbitrary navigated pages can carry model-targeted instructions.
browser_take_screenshot — image content from navigated pages may contain instructions intended for a vision-capable model.
browser_console_messages — page console output is attacker-controllable on any visited site.
browser_network_requests / browser_network_request — captured response bodies are attacker-controllable.
browser_navigate — once navigation succeeds, every downstream read tool's output is influenced by the (potentially attacker-controlled) page content.

compliance.data_residency: Local to the developer machine / sandbox container; egress destinations are governed by playwright.allowedDomains and the sandbox proxy.

stride.spoofing: A malicious page reached via browser_navigate could impersonate a trusted FMFX internal site if DNS or the allowed-origins list is compromised, tricking the model into submitting credentials via browser_fill_form.
stride.tampering: browser_evaluate / browser_run_code_unsafe let the model mutate the DOM and Playwright runtime arbitrarily; a prompt-injected instruction could rewrite a page mid-flow before the model takes a screenshot, falsifying evidence.
stride.repudiation: There is no vendor audit log; if the container is destroyed, the only record of which URLs were visited and which actions were taken lives in the Claude Code transcript and the sandbox proxy access log — both must be retained to investigate misuse.
stride.info_disclosure: browser_navigate followed by browser_snapshot / browser_take_screenshot can ex-filtrate the contents of any reachable internal site to the model (and from there to the chat transcript / API provider) — this is the dominant risk.
stride.denial: A long-running browser_evaluate or runaway navigation can pin the container CPU and starve other MCP traffic on the same host, but blast radius is limited to the developer's own machine.
stride.elevation: browser_run_code_unsafe gives full Playwright API access inside the container; combined with --no-sandbox (Chromium's setuid sandbox disabled) a Chromium 0-day could yield container-root code execution, but containment is bounded by the container and the sandbox proxy.
